description: Test procedures for AU-2 Event Logging

output.objective: Verify that the organization identifies the types of events that the system is capable of logging, and that logging is configured to capture those event types for audit purposes.

steps.procedure: Review the organization's audit logging policy and compare against the AU-2 event types: authentication events (success/failure), privileged access, object access, policy changes, account management, process tracking. Verify the policy was approved within the last 12 months.
steps.pass_criteria: Policy exists, is approved, and lists at least the 6 required AU-2 event categories.
steps.fail_criteria: Policy missing, unapproved, older than 12 months, or omits event categories.

steps.procedure: Perform a test login with a valid credential on a production system. Then perform a deliberate failed login. Retrieve the audit log and verify both events are captured with: timestamp, user identity, event type, source IP, and outcome.
steps.pass_criteria: Both events present in logs within 5 seconds. All required fields populated.
steps.fail_criteria: Events absent, delayed >60s, or missing required fields.

steps.procedure: Interview the system administrator responsible for logging configuration. Ask: (1) How are logging requirements communicated to development teams? (2) What is the process for adding new event types? (3) How are logging failures detected?
steps.pass_criteria: Administrator can demonstrate awareness of event requirements and escalation process for logging failures.
steps.fail_criteria: Administrator unaware of logging requirements or cannot describe failure detection.

expected_results.pass: All three test procedures produce evidence of appropriate event capture, policy coverage, and staff awareness.
expected_results.fail: One or more procedures reveal missing event types, policy gaps, or staff unfamiliarity with requirements.

output.sample_size: Select 3 production systems; test login events on each
output.frequency: Annual + after any system change that affects logging configuration
evidence_to_collect: Log export showing test authentication events with all required fields

description: Test procedures for IA-5 Authenticator Management (password complexity)

output.objective: Verify that authenticator management practices enforce minimum password complexity, prohibit reuse, and require rotation in accordance with IA-5(1).

steps.procedure: Review the password policy in the identity provider (Active Directory, Okta, Cognito). Verify: minimum length ≥ 12 characters, complexity requirements (uppercase, lowercase, digit, special char), prohibition of last 10 passwords, maximum age ≤ 365 days.
steps.pass_criteria: All four parameters meet or exceed NIST SP 800-63B and IA-5(1) requirements.

steps.procedure: Attempt to set a non-compliant password (e.g., 8 lowercase characters). Verify the system rejects it with an informative error. Attempt to reuse the last password after a forced reset. Verify rejection.
steps.pass_criteria: Both non-compliant attempts are rejected with clear user feedback.

expected_results.pass: Policy configuration meets IA-5(1) requirements and enforcement is confirmed through testing.
expected_results.fail: Policy below threshold or enforcement can be bypassed.

output.sample_size: Test on primary production IdP; spot-check 2 application-level password policies
output.frequency: Annual + after any IdP configuration change
evidence_to_collect: Password policy configuration screenshot (IdP admin console)
Screen recording or screenshots of failed non-compliant password attempts
